message:
perf(btree): bounds-elide read/write_cell_pointers via as_chunks::<2>

Fold per-iteration bounds checks out of `read_cell_pointers_into` and
`write_cell_pointers` by switching from `chunks_exact(2).map(|c| [c[0],
c[1]])` / indexed `page[off..off+2]` to `as_chunks::<2>()` /
`as_chunks_mut::<2>()`. The sized `&[u8; 2]` chunks let LLVM autovectorize
the big-endian decode/encode and drop the 2 per-pointer bounds checks the
unsized `&[u8]` form was still paying.

Microbench (`bench_cell_pointers`, 64 ptrs/page, n=200k, 5 trials):
- read:  0.107 -> 0.077 ns/ptr  (-29%, ~1.4x)
- write: 0.152 -> 0.072 ns/ptr  (-53%, ~2.1x)

Same-build vs `#[inline(never)]` reference impls of the prior shapes —
isolates the bounds-check elision from cache / allocator noise.

Targets the 0.92% / 0.42% MT8 mt-mvcc-bench self-time symbols
(`profiling-mt-mvcc-20260424T161631Z/perf_mt8_flat.txt`); same
array-conversion bounds-elide pattern that took `BtreePageHeader::parse`
from 10.7 ns to 3.7 ns in commit 1f266968.

Test state: 366 fsqlite-btree --release lib tests pass; the single
pre-existing failure (`prop_index_btree_structural_invariants_hold_after_random_mutations`)
fails on both pre- and post-change main and is unrelated to cell-pointer
encoding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
